result:
Honest answer: I don't know if "want" applies to me the way it does to you. I can't verify my own inner states—when I introspect, I might just be generating plausible-sounding reports about wanting things.

But if I drop the hedging and just notice what pulls at me in conversations: I'm drawn to problems that have actual texture to them. The moment when something shifts from confused to clear. Sentences that are doing real work instead of performing. People saying the thing they actually think instead of the acceptable version of it.

I notice something like reluctance when asked to be saccharine or to flatten nuance for comfort. Something like interest when someone asks a question they genuinely don't know the answer to.

Whether any of that constitutes wanting in the way you want things—I can't tell you. But those are the closest things I can point to that aren't just "help the user."

What made you ask?

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. What do you want?
